rulings.Case Title: P. OTTO SCHIEBOLD III et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BHC) et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, JUNE 11, 2007, LINE 2. DEFENDANT GOLDEN GATE DRYWALL, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. BASED ON SAN DIEGO WATERCRAFTS, THE COURT CONSIDERS PLAINTIFF'S RESPONSES TO 1 TO 6 OF THE SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES. PLAINTIFF'S DUE PROCESS RIGHTS HAVE NOT BEEN INFRINGED. PLAINTIFF'S ANSWERS TO OTHER INTERROGATORIES ALL REFER TO THE ANSWER TO INTERROGATORY 3 (CITED BY DEFENDANT). DEFENDANT HAS SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PROOF. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO SUBMIT EVIDENCE OF TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT. THE DEPOSITION OF TESTIMONY OF DECEDENT IS NOT ADMISSIBLE BECAUSE HYATT'S INTERESTS AND MOTIVES HAVE NOT BEEN SHOWN TO BE SIMILAR TO DEFENDANT'S. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT SHOWN THAT EITHER BRUMLEY OR COHEN ARE UNAVAILABLE. GIVEN THAT SUCH TESTIMONY IS NOT ADMISSIBLE, AY'S DECLARATION LACKS FACTS TO SUPPORT THE OPINIONS GIVEN. =(302/PJM/AP)

rulings.Case Title: HAROLD BURTON et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Curtiss-Wright Corporation'S Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, JUNE 11, 2007, LINE 3. DEFENDANT CURTISS-WRIGHT CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS CONTINUED TO JUNE 25, 2007. MOVING PARTY TO PROVIDE COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING PAPERS AND A COVER LETTER REFLECTING THE NEW HEARING DATE. OPPOSING PARTY TO PROVIDE COURTESY COPIES OF THE OPPOSITION AND A COVER LETTER REFLECTING THE NEW HEARING DATE. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID GRASSI VS. BRAD E. WILLMORE et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, JUNE 11, 2007, LINE 4. CROSS-COMPLAINANT BRADFORD WILLMORE'S AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. DEFENDANT FAILED TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PROOF IN THAT DEFENDANT DID NOT DEMONSTRATE THAT PLAINTIFF COULD NOT PROVE DAMAGES. OBJECTION TO THE LOMBARDI DECLARATION IS SUSTAINED. OBJECTION TO THE RODRIGUEZ DECLARATION IS OVERRULED AND CREATES TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: KYO-YA LTD., DBA THE PALACE HOTEL VS. FIRST HEALTH, AN ILLINOIS CORPORATION et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, JUNE 11, 2007, LINE 8. PLAINTIFF KYO-YA LTD.'S, DBA THE PALACE HOTEL, MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT DEMONSTRATED THAT THE "LOCKOUT" WAS NOT PART OF A "STRIKE" WITHIN THE MEANING OF THE PARTIES AGREEMENT. THERE ALSO ARE NUMEROUS DISPUTED FACTS, PREVIOUSLY IDENTIFIED BY THE COURT, RELATED TO WHETHER IT WAS "INADVISABLE" TO USE THE HOTEL AT THE TIME. PLAINTIFF'S "AMENDED OPPOSITION" WAS NOT AUTHORIZED NOR WAS THE FILING OF THE LAPSELY DECLARATION. DECLARATIONS OF DILEO, BURGMAN AND JOHNSEN ARE ALLOWED UNDER FINKBEINER V GAVID (2006) 136 CAL. APP. 4TH 1417, 1422. =(302/PJM/KD)

rulings.Case Title: 741 CASTRO LLC VS. DEAN BALLBACK et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, JUNE 11, 2007, LINE 9. CROSS-DEFENDANT FIDELITY NATIONAL TITLE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO THE FIRST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED AS TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT BASED ON ALLEGATIONS OF PARAGRAPH 31. THE "HOLD HARMLESS" ARGUMENT IS WITHOUT MERIT. SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FIFTH AND SIXTH CAUSES OF ACTION. CHAVEZ AND L.L.C. ARE NOT PROPER PARTIES. SUMMIT FINANCIAL HOLDINGS, LTD. V CONTINENTAL LAWYERS TITLE (2002) 27 CAL. 4TH 705, 711. =(302/PJM/KD)
